--- 
title: "Introductory Programming in R"
author: "Bao Maddux"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
# url: your book url like https://bookdown.org/yihui/bookdown
# cover-image: path to the social sharing image like images/cover.jpg
description: |
  This is a minimal example of using the bookdown package to write a book.
  The HTML output format for this example is bookdown::gitbook,
  set in the _output.yml file.
link-citations: yes
github-repo: rstudio/bookdown-demo
---

# About R and RStudio


## What is R and RStudio?
**R** is a language and environment for statistical computing and graphics. It is a GNU project which is similar to the S language and environment which was developed at Bell Laboratories (formerly AT&T, now Lucent Technologies) by John Chambers and colleagues. R can be considered as a different implementation of S. There are some important differences, but much code written for S runs unaltered under R.

**R** provides a wide variety of statistical (linear and nonlinear modelling, classical statistical tests, time-series analysis, classification, clustering, …) and graphical techniques, and is highly extensible. The S language is often the vehicle of choice for research in statistical methodology, and R provides an Open Source route to participation in that activity.

**RStudio** is an integrated development environment for R, a programming language for statistical computing and graphics. **RStudio** offers a copious number of other nice features and a clean editor with syntax highlighting.

## How to Install R and RStudio?
